problem_statement: Mid-size e-commerce brands struggle with fragmented, manual returns processes — generating return labels, processing refunds, updating inventory, and disposing of returned goods costs 20-65% of item value and generates 2.4 support interactions per return, while returned inventory worth billions sits idle or gets liquidated at deep discounts.
target_user: Mid-size e-commerce brands (D2C, Shopify/WooCommerce merchants doing $1M-$50M/year) whose return volumes (150-2000+/month) exceed what manual processes can handle but who lack enterprise budgets for solutions like Narvar.

current_solutions.solution: Shopify draft orders + discount codes for exchanges

evidence_summary: 40+ unique complaint threads found across Shopify community forums, G2/Capterra reviews, Shopify App Store reviews (Loop: 23 one-star out of 411), and industry articles with merchant quotes. Pattern: merchants universally cite fragmented tooling, manual label generation, slow refund processing (up to 2 weeks manually), and no integrated path for returned inventory resale. The $890B returns market generates processing costs of 20-65% of item value, with 30%+ of returned items unable to be resold as new. 93% of merchants report return fraud/abuse as a substantial challenge.
